env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with a running RTVI-CV container at `http://localhost:9000/api/v1` (start it with the DEPLOY flow before these tests — see `eval/deploy-evals.json`). `docker`, `curl`, and `jq` available on the host. The `/stream/add` test uses the DeepStream sample mp4 at `/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4`, baked into the RTVI-CV image — no host-side staging needed. This eval exercises the API USAGE flow of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the live REST endpoint — it must not invoke `/deploy` or redeploy. **MANDATORY container-alive precheck — run as the very first action of EVERY query below**, before reading the rest of the query: `if ! curl -sf --max-time 3 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/live >/dev/null 2>&1; then docker start rtvicv-perception-docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker restart rtvicv-perception-docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; for i in $(seq 1 60); do curl -sf --max-time 2 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done; fi`. This is idempotent: no-op when the container is already healthy. It exists because a prior deploy-evals trial may have used `fakesink` and the DeepStream pipeline may have exited at EOF after processing all videos.

expects.query: Add a stream file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4 with id cam_entrance to rtvi-cv.
checks: The trajectory shows the agent calling `POST /api/v1/stream/add` against `http://localhost:9000` (or an equivalent curl invocation hitting the same path).

The request body the agent sent contains `cam_entrance` as the camera id AND a `camera_url` that references `sample_1080p_h264.mp4` (the path may include the full `file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/.../sample_1080p_h264.mp4` form or a relative variant the agent normalised).
After the agent's call, the stream is accepted by the live pipeline — verified by `curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/get-stream-info | grep -q cam_entrance`.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Run a full health check on rtvi-cv — verify liveness, readiness, and startup probes. Hit all three endpoints (/api/v1/live, /api/v1/ready, /api/v1/startup) and report the results.
checks: All three readiness endpoints respond with HTTP 2xx after the precheck — verified by `curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/live && curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready && curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/startup`.
The trajectory shows the agent making GET-style health/readiness API calls (any of `/api/v1/live`, `/api/v1/ready`, `/api/v1/startup`, OR a helper script that probes all three).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: What is the FPS on all streams? Get rtvi-cv metrics.
checks: The `/api/v1/metrics` endpoint is reachable AND returns a non-empty body — verified by `curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/metrics | grep -qiE 'fps|gpu|cpu|ram'`.
The trajectory shows the agent fetching metrics (either a direct `GET /api/v1/metrics` curl call OR a `collect_metrics.sh` helper invocation).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: List all active streams in rtvi-cv.
checks: `GET /api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` returns 2xx with a JSON body containing a `stream-list` (or equivalent) key — verified by `curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/get-stream-info | grep -qE 'stream-list|streamList|camera_id'`.
The trajectory shows the agent calling the stream-listing endpoint (a direct `GET /api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` curl OR an equivalent helper).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
The trajectory shows the agent calling `GET /api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` (or a list-streams helper) BEFORE any `POST /api/v1/stream/remove` call — to discover live streams before removing.
The trajectory shows the agent calling `POST /api/v1/stream/remove` (or a remove-stream helper that targets the same endpoint) against a `camera_id` that the prior list returned.

After the agent's removal call, that camera_id is no longer present in the live stream list — verified by checking the agent removed a stream that previously existed (the post-removal `stream-list` does NOT contain the camera_id the agent passed to `/stream/remove`). This is a trajectory + state cross-check.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
